mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential (mean return < 0.1%) compared to baseline periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in near-term earnings beats.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >50% of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of high-inflation months, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.2 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market that supports consumer income and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative mean forward return over 60 days, or its predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample classification tasks.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated quarterly GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening to prevent overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of late-cycle high-GDP observations, or the mechanism is indistinguishable from random noise with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate indicates a synchronized supply-labor recovery that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative return differential relative to baseline, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate models.
